description: Phase 1: Expanded benchmarks 40->120 (30/topic). Added metadata fields. Discovered 3 hint leak vulnerabilities.

Iteration 6: Independent answer verifier + semantic quality checks. Mutation kill rate 8.3% -> 41.7%. Fixed average generator IEEE 754 float division. Added answer_verifier.py with 4 topic verifiers + mathematical invariants. Integrated into benchmark runner, mutation testing, and full cycle.

Iteration 7: Wording consistency validator + average template fix. Fixed template_index=0 hardcoded san-ci (3 exams) -> uses {n} for actual count. New wording_validator.py checks problem text matches parameters. Removed stale quality check. Integrated into benchmark runner and mutation testing.

Iteration 8: Fraction mutation kill rate 10%% -> 30%%. Added 2 output-based quality checks to mutator: (1) single_denominator_one_not_fraction catches mutations that simplify a fraction to a whole number (den=1 after generator simplification), (2) lcd_too_complex_for_grade catches mutations producing LCD > 24 (too complex for grade 5). Overall mutation kill rate 40.8%% -> 47.5%%. +8 kills, all from fraction topic.

Iteration 9: Equivalent mutation detection + unit_conversion quality hardening. (1) Added equivalent mutation detector: flags mutations that produce the exact same answer as the original benchmark case (e.g. swap_ab for commutative addition, template_shift that only changes wording). Caught 24 equivalent mutations. (2) Added value_one_reverse_trivial check for unit_conversion (1 small-unit -> trivially small decimal). (3) Added conversion_answer_too_large safety net (>= 100000). Overall mutation kill rate 47.5%% -> 64.2%%. +20 kills. Gold candidates 1 -> 9.

Iteration 10: Average mutation kill rate 84%% -> 100%% (first topic at 100%%). Added too_few_values_for_average quality check: mutations that reduce value count to < 3 produce trivially easy problems. +4 net kills. Overall mutation kill rate 64.2%% -> 67.5%%. Remaining 39 survivors are mostly genuinely valid alternative problems — system approaching natural mutation testing plateau.

Iteration 11: Difficulty drift detection. Added cross-cutting _estimate_difficulty() function that computes problem difficulty from input parameters (fraction: LCD-based, decimal: decimal places + op, average: count + spread, unit_conversion: multiplier + direction). Mutations causing >3x difficulty change flagged as quality:difficulty_drift. Fraction kill rate 47.5%% -> 70.0%% (+22.5%%). Unit conversion 60.0%% -> 66.7%%. Overall 67.5%% -> 76.7%% (+9.2%%). +11 net kills.

Iteration 12: Fraction numerator mutations + zero-operand quality check. Added 3 new mutation operators to _mutate_fraction: a_num_zero, b_num_zero, a_num_equals_den. These test numerator perturbations (previously only denominators were mutated). Added quality:zero_fraction_operand check. All 15 new mutations killed (15/15). Bonus discovery: zero-numerator mutations also trigger L3 hint leaks (trivial answer appears in hint text). Fraction 70.0%% -> 78.2%%. Overall 76.7%% -> 79.3%%. Gold candidates 10 -> 12.

Iteration 13: Answer decimal places quality check for unit_conversion. Added quality:too_many_answer_decimal_places — answers with >2 decimal places are inappropriate for grade 5. Killed 7 additional mutations: all 3 direction_flip survivors (0.003, 0.025, 0.004), both value_huge survivors (99.999), and 2 decimal_value reverse survivors (0.0005). Unit conversion 66.7%% -> 90.0%% (+23.3%%). Overall 79.3%% -> 84.4%% (+5.1%%). Gold candidates 12 -> 17.
